questions.question: Which of the following drawing tools is used to draw parallel lines and vertical lines quickly?
questions.explanation: A T-square is used primarily as a guide for drawing horizontal lines and as a base for set squares to draw vertical and inclined lines.

questions.question: A scale of 1:5 is an enlarging scale.
questions.explanation: 1:5 is a reducing scale because the drawing size is smaller than the actual object size.

questions.question: In First Angle Projection, where is the object placed?
options: C) Between the observer and planes
questions.explanation: In First Angle Projection, the object is assumed to be in the first quadrant, situated between the observer and the planes of projection.

questions.question: Which type of line is used to represent hidden edges of an object?
questions.explanation: Dashed lines (short dashes) are standard for representing edges or outlines that are not visible from a specific view.

questions.question: If a plane cuts a cone parallel to its base, the resulting section is a:
questions.explanation: When the cutting plane is perpendicular to the axis (parallel to the base), the section is a circle.

questions.question: Dimension lines should generally cross each other to save space.
questions.explanation: Dimension lines should never cross each other or extension lines if avoidable, to maintain clarity.

questions.question: The front view of an object is projected onto the:
questions.explanation: The front view (elevation) is always projected onto the Vertical Plane.

questions.question: What is the standard size of an A4 drawing sheet in mm?
questions.explanation: Standard A4 dimensions are 210mm width by 297mm height.

questions.question: The height to width ratio for most standard engineering uppercase letters is usually:
questions.explanation: Standard engineering lettering (B-type) often follows a 7:4 or 10:7 ratio for most letters.

questions.question: Projectors are always parallel to each other in orthographic projection.
questions.explanation: In orthographic projection, the projectors are parallel to each other and perpendicular to the projection plane.

questions.question: What is the Representative Fraction (RF) if a distance of 10 meters is represented by 2 centimeters?
questions.explanation: RF = Drawing Distance / Actual Distance. 2cm / 1000cm = 1/500.

questions.question: A point lies 20mm above HP and 30mm behind VP. In which quadrant is the point located?
questions.explanation: Above HP and Behind VP corresponds to the Second Quadrant.

questions.question: What curve is generated by a point on the circumference of a circle rolling along a straight line without slipping?
questions.explanation: A cycloid is the locus of a point on the rim of a circle rolling along a straight line.

questions.question: If a line is parallel to both HP and VP, its projections will appear as:
options: B) True length in both views
C) True length in FV, point in TV
questions.explanation: When a line is parallel to a plane, its projection on that plane shows the true length.

questions.question: In isometric projection, all three axes are inclined at 120 degrees to each other.
questions.explanation: Isometric axes are drawn 120 degrees apart so that the object appears equally foreshortened.

questions.question: When the eccentricity (e) of a conic section is exactly 1, the curve is a:
questions.explanation: e < 1 is an ellipse, e = 1 is a parabola, and e > 1 is a hyperbola.

questions.question: What is the purpose of drawing 'hatching' lines in a technical drawing?
questions.explanation: Hatching (section lines) is used to show the surface that has been cut by a section plane.

questions.question: When a plane is perpendicular to HP and parallel to VP, its Top View will be a:
questions.explanation: Since the plane is perpendicular to the HP, its projection on the HP (Top View) will be a line.

questions.question: Isometric scale is used in an Isometric View.
questions.explanation: Isometric Scale is used in Isometric Projection. In Isometric View, actual dimensions are used.

questions.question: In AutoCAD, which command is used to round the corners of a rectangle?
questions.explanation: The Fillet command creates a rounded internal or external corner between two 2D objects.

questions.question: A line AB has its end A in HP and 20mm in front of VP. The line is inclined at 30° to HP and 45° to VP. The length of the FV will be:
questions.explanation: The length of the Front View (Plan length) is the projection of the true length onto the VP, which is calculated as L*cos(φ), where φ is the inclination with VP.

questions.question: What is the shape of the development of the lateral surface of a cylinder?
questions.explanation: When unrolled, the lateral surface of a cylinder forms a rectangle with width equal to the circumference (2πr) and height equal to the cylinder's height.

questions.question: A right circular cone is resting on its generator on the HP. Its axis will be:
questions.explanation: When a cone rests on its generator, the axis is automatically inclined to the plane on which the generator lies.

questions.question: What is the ratio of Isometric Length to True Length?
questions.explanation: Isometric length = True length * cos(45°)/cos(30°) ≈ 0.816 * True length.

questions.question: The line of intersection between two cylinders of different diameters intersecting at right angles is always a straight line.
questions.explanation: The intersection of two cylinders usually results in a curve. It only becomes a straight line if both cylinders have equal diameters.

questions.question: What curve is traced by a point on a circle rolling on the *inside* of another circle?
questions.explanation: Rolling on the outside produces an epicycloid; rolling on the inside produces a hypocycloid.

questions.question: If a regular pentagon plane is inclined to HP and perpendicular to VP, what is the shape of its Front View?
questions.explanation: Since the plane is perpendicular to the Vertical Plane, its projection on the VP (Front View) will collapse into a straight line inclined at the same angle as the plane.

questions.question: An Auxiliary Vertical Plane (AVP) is:
options: B) Perpendicular to both HP and VP
C) Inclined to VP and Perpendicular to HP
D) Inclined to HP and Perpendicular to VP
questions.explanation: An AVP is a plane perpendicular to HP but inclined to VP.

questions.question: In perspective projection, the 'Station Point' represents the eye of the observer.
questions.explanation: The station point is the location of the observer's eye from which the object is being viewed.

questions.question: The 'Radial Line Method' of development is used for which of the following?
questions.explanation: Prisms and cylinders use Parallel Line Development; Pyramids and Cones use Radial Line Development.
